City Transit Board Approves Overnight Service Pilot

By Maya Chen | February 11, 2026

After a two-hour public hearing, the regional transit board approved a six-month overnight service pilot for the Blue and Green rail lines. The vote followed testimony from restaurant workers, hospital staff, and airport employees who said late-night travel options currently disappear before many shifts end.

Transit planners said the pilot will run on Friday and Saturday nights, with trains every 20 minutes between midnight and 4 a.m. The agency will track ridership, station safety, and transfer reliability, then publish monthly updates to determine whether the schedule should become permanent.

Board chair Elena Ruiz called the decision a practical response to changing work patterns. She said the plan balances access and cost by focusing on corridors where employers report the highest concentration of overnight staff. A coalition of neighborhood groups that once opposed expansion said better staffing and lighting commitments helped secure support.

The first trains under the pilot are scheduled for March, and the agency expects to release a final recommendation before the winter budget cycle. Advocates described the vote as a turning point in the long campaign for dependable, all-hours transit mobility.
